LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, ACTING BY AND THROUGH DMV, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - CONTINUED TO JUNE 3, 2011 ON COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998
MINI-MINUTES FOR MAY-17-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, ACTING BY AND THROUGH DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES DEMURRER (TO COMPLAINT). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT DVM IS OPERATIONALLY SIMILAR TO A SCHOOL DISTRICT (BOTH INTERACT DIRECTLY WITH THE PUBLIC AND PROVIDES AN AFFIRMATIVE SERVICE TO THE PUBLIC) AND DISSIMILAR TO A PRISON. ACCORDINGLY THE COURT RELIES ON NICOLE M. BY AND THROUGH JACQUELINE M. V. MARTINEZ UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT., N.D. CAL. 1997, 964 F.SUPP.1369 WHICH HELD THAT A SCHOOL DISTRICT IS A "BUSINESS ESTABLISHMENT" FOR PURPOSES OF THE UNRUH CIVIL RIGHTS ACT. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT THE ARGUMENT THAT THE DMV IS A BUSINESS ESTABLISHMENT TO SURVIVE A DEMURRER. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS TO SHOW THAT THE HARM SHE SUFFERED WAS FORESEEABLE WITH THE SCOPE OF DEMARTINI'S EMPLOYMENT WITH THE DMV. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. THESE ARE PROPERLY PLEAD AND PRESENT QUESTIONS OF FACT THAT ARE NOT APPROPRIATE FOR DETERMINATION AT THIS STAGE OF THE PROCEEDINGS. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. IN ADDITION, THE COURT STATED THAT PLAINTIFF HAS 60 DAYS TO FILE THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.) (302)
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUN-03-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Chris Dolan and Vanessa Wills for and with Amber Yust (Plaintiff). Tom Blake and Rosemarie Ruggieri for Department of Moter Vehicles. Danniel Cano and Martin Zurada for and with Thomas DeMartini. Kristina Wertz for Transgender Law Center.

Mediation was conducted. Mr. Dolan cited the terms of the settlement agreement for the record. Ms. Ruggieri also stated some of the lauguage included in the settlement agreement. All parties concur with the terms of the settlement. Notice of conditional settlement will be filed with Court.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, ACTING BY AND THROUGH DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES DEMURRER (TO COMPLAINT).

APPEARANCES: Vanessa K. Wills, Esq. of Dolan Law Firm 421-2800 for pltf & opposing party; Tom Blake, Deputy Atty General 703-5506 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant's Demurrer to the 1st cause of action is overruled. The Court finds that Defendant DVM is operationally similar to a School District (both interact directly with the public and provides an affirmative service to the public) and dissimilar to a prison. Accordingly the Court relies on Nicole M. By and Through Jacqueline M. v. Martinez Unified School District., N.D. Cal. 1997, 964 F.Supp.1369 which held that a school district is a "business establishment" for purposes of the Unruh Civil Rights Act. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to support the argument that the DMV is a business establishment to survive a Demurrer. Defendant's Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to show that the harm she suffered was foreseeable with the scope of DeMartini's employment with the DMV.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 3rd and 4th causes of action is overruled. These are properly plead and present questions of fact that are not appropriate for determination at this stage of the proceedings.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 5th cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. In addition, the Court stated that plaintiff has 60 days to file the first amended complaint from notice of entry of order. (Moving party to prepare a proposed form of order.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
